item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is Damnbi supposed to be scp 2845?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this one! He seems like a pretty cool dude. Wouldn't mind going out for ice cream with him. ;3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is one of the first normal SCPs (outside of joke-SCPs) that actually made me laugh. I thoroughly enjoyed reading this SCP, from the special containment procedures to the final addendum. Also, this is surprisingly touching, even if the stories are morbid (but given they're written by The Lord of All Evil that's understandable). Not to mention, this reminds me of the Chinese perception of harmony in diversity with "there's no evil in good else good is no good." Best read I've had in a while.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's a nice relaxing story that's perfect for a change of pace. Upvoted.

quick question
just curious

Is this Clef's father-in-law?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"It is mentioned many times throughout that GROG is sleepy."

"mentioned many times"

Ah screw you satan T.T givin me ptsd

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted. Honestly, this is just such a delightful SCP. I love how the interview is written too—every line of 2980's dialogue sounds like it was ripped from an indie author promoting his new book. Especially when 2980 casually plugs his new book.

I think it's fitting that among all these strange, unexplainable creatures in the SCP Foundation, Actual Satan is just doing his own thing.

I also like the idea that 2980 just released that censored SCP for writing inspiration.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You're free not to like something, but I think you're confused on a couple points here:

I thought there is a specific designation for that.

You're thinking of the Joke SCPs, but the idea is that -J's are parody articles, not just SCPs that happen to be mildly humorous. Over-the-top lampoons poking fun at classic articles and site tropes, community in-jokes, things written as in-universe copypasta within the Foundation, that sort of thing.

that one skip that keeps telling researchers all the methods for its containment instead of escaping

Ironically enough, I'm pretty sure you're thinking of SCP-4357-J, which is one the better examples of a cartoonishly silly -J. Contrast the subdued humor here and I think you'll see the difference.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hm. Someone in an earlier post stated that "There is nothing wrong with funny". And that's right but I thought there is a specific designation for that. I'd certainly give it a + if it was a -J but as it is supposed to be a mainlist article… no vote from me.

I'm perfectly fine with moderate levels of "sillyness" in mainlist articles, like that one skip that keeps telling researchers all the methods for its containment instead of escaping but this just feels too much.

item.title: Wikiwalk Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
Edit: edits made

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ Show proposed edit
- Hide

To link to other site-81 content and a referenced skip, I propose the following edits:

SCP-2980 is to be contained in a standard item locker at Site-81.

to

SCP-2980 is to be contained in a standard item locker at Site-81.

and

Story depicts the main character, GROG THE UNSPEAKABLE,

to

Story depicts the main character, GROG THE UNSPEAKABLE,

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I want Satan to read me a bedtime story.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You know, there's a movie of this skip. Two actually.
The Devil's Carnival and its sequel. The framing device of the first movie is that the devil is reading Aesop's fables to a kid (in a universe where Aesop's fables appear to be the defining codex of morality. Don't be trusting kids, or you're going to Hell).
Practical upshot of this is that I'm hearing Terrence Zdunich's voice when reading this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
ADORABLE!

There is nothing wrong with funny. Doesn't hurt that I hear the voice of Satan from Southpark for the interviews.

+1. Awesome work. Would love to read a writeup of some of the actual stories. (would also love a list of references, but one can't ask too much, no?)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Satan.
Reading bedtime stories.
I'm sorry. I need a moment.
To upvote this masterpiece.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Still love this article every time I read it.

Fun fact: this page has a word count of 6661 characters. Truly, this is the work of Satan.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Satan takes a break from soul tormenting and becomes a hipster children's book writer. I'm not sure what to think of this. If it wasn't literal "red guy with hooves and horns" Satan and rather some other monstrosity (or a different form of Satan), I might dig it more. That part makes it a little too tongue-in-cheek.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this SCP. I found a new favorite one. Good job!

+1

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded: The scarlet king isn't all that bad when you get to know him

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I saw the name and thought to myself 'yea its a nightlight which is tots gonna make a monster or some shit and its gonna eat kids while they sleep'
but it reads fucking stories to you
while wearing spectacles

Damnbi

+1
The Prince of Darkness this wiki needs and deserves.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: One of my new favorites. I just get the mental image of Satan, with reading glasses and a mug of coffee of course, being interviewed on Good Morning America about his life's work……children's bedtime stories.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I admit that I did a quick double-check to see if it was a -J, but I'm glad it's not.

It's funny, more than silly. And I think that is what makes the difference between mainlister and -J. The fact that it is very well written, with both -1 and the interviewer having their own voices is what tipped it into +1 territory for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's too.. heartwarming. It's too funny. It's too perfect in every conceivable way.

Some will find it 'too silly' but those people are dumb.

+1 so hard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I absolutely love this :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well done, DjKaktus. Once again you've created an intriguing skip. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
To me this article also danced very close to the line of 'too silly' but ultimately it was funny, heartwarming, and potentially worrisome enough that I really couldn't do anything but upvote.

Oh, and don't think I haven't been taking notice of your skips, djkaktus. Your work is excellent for one so new.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel that this article was on the verge of being too silly for me, but I really, really enjoyed it. Good job. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This article seems silly, but I know people who talk this way, so why can't a demon author of children's books? Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's written well and I do think it's genuinely amusing, so you should definitely feel good about doing something with merit to it. This is just too silly for me as a mainlister, though, so I have to vote in this case based strictly on personal preference.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Oh absolutely. A lot of it is drawn from personal experiences, stuff I encounter on a day to day basis. Pretty personal, you know. I also dabble in a lot of different works. Read a lot of different stuff by different authors. Really try and widen my horizons, right? Get a good feel of what the kids are reading nowadays."

"And it was at that moment that the god of upvotes called upon me to upvote this masterpiece."
But in all seriousness the SCP is funny but in a strange, and dark way… and that's plenty enough for an upvote. Furthermore this is easily one of my new favorite SCPs of all time. :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, yeah, a tales series covering full versions of those stories would be amazing.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What in the actual hell did I just read? Seriously +666.
I know cross testing is a major faux pas these days, but can't we bring 343 to just one reading?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You know, I loved the skip's personality, liked the possible stories, and damn! I'd buy the book if I could. And even seriously thinking about writing those stories in my sandbox just to see what happens. Yeah, I like this a lot. +1

E: I feel like that last addendum should be in a different format, but that's my personal taste. Still too cute to downvote it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is too silly for me, and not terribly interesting. It's too winking to be a mainlister, but not funny enough to be a -J.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For some reason I can't help but picture the demon here as a sort of younger, modern Mister Rogers. Which is *the best thing*.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was going to complain that we already have a nightlight that summons a humanoid when turned on, but then it got to Satan reading bedtime stories and I just fell in love. This demon is so damned personable, and I just love this whole thing to death.

Also did you reference one of my SCPs oh my god :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll only admit that there is more than one reference in there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it. Is the "Grog" one a reference to SCP-1435?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Lost it at the story samples.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This straddles the line between joke and mainlister. I think this could go either way. I like it, but if you get a lot of complaints, don't be afraid to ham it up a bit and tack a -J on the end!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is silly, but that's good thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey everybody! Thanks again for the tons of support I've gotten for this. To everybody who read it, critiqued it, looked it over in chat, I really appreciate it.
